questions.question: In an ER diagram, total participation is represented using:
Which property of a relation ensures that no two tuples are identical?

Predict the output (CODE('L') = 76, CODE('E') = 69, CODE('V') = 86; non-zero is true):

S = "LEVEL"
SET count = 0
FOR i = 1 TO 5
  IF ((CODE(S[i]) mod 2) && (S[i] = S[6 - i])) THEN
    count = count + 1
  ENDIF
ENDFOR
PRINT count
Why are integrity constraints important in database systems?
options: They help maintain valid and accurate data
They help by reducing query execution time
questions.correctAnswer: They help maintain valid and accurate data

questions.question: Which relationship type typically requires the creation of a separate associative entity during database design?
Which operation produces all possible combinations of tuples from two relations?

Predict the output:

S = "AABBBC"
SET T = ""
SET i = 1
WHILE i <= 6
  SET c = 1
  WHILE (i < 6) AND (S[i] = S[i + 1])
    c = c + 1
    i = i + 1
  ENDWHILE
  T = T + S[i] + c
  i = i + 1
ENDWHILE
PRINT T
In the relational model, degree of a relation refers to:
options: Number of tuples in a relation
Number of attributes in a relation
questions.correctAnswer: Number of attributes in a relation

questions.question:
Predict the output (note: there are more than usual spaces between "AI" and "IS"):

S = "AI  IS THE FUTURE"
SET count = 0
FOR i = 1 TO LENGTH(S)
    IF S[i] = " " THEN
        count = count + 1
    ENDIF
ENDFOR
PRINT count + 1
What is the main purpose of cardinality constraints in ER modeling?

Predict the output( string is 1-indexed ):

SET S = ""
FOR i = 1 TO 3
    S = S + "AB"
ENDFOR
PRINT LENGTH(S) + 2
